_meta.description: Key doctrinal academic references supporting NormTrace methodology. Organised by theoretical domain. These are the primary scholarly sources underpinning the analytical framework.

doctrine.title: Demarginalising the intersection of race and sex: a black feminist critique of antidiscrimination doctrine, feminist theory and antiracist politics
doctrine.relevance: Foundational conceptualisation of intersectionality as a methodology for identifying how multiple identity axes interact to produce specific forms of discrimination; directly applied in NormTrace Stage 2 intersectionality analysis.

doctrine.title: Human rights and disability: the current use and future potential of United Nations human rights instruments in the context of disability
doctrine.relevance: Comprehensive mapping of the pre-CRPD international human rights framework applied to disability; establishes the conceptual shift from welfare to rights model that the CRPD codifies.

doctrine.title: Out of darkness into light? Introducing the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities
doctrine.relevance: Authoritative early commentary on the CRPD's normative structure and the shift to the social-human rights model of disability; essential reference for interpreting CRPD Art. 3 and Art. 12.

doctrine.title: Legal capacity in the disability rights convention: stranglehold of the past or lodestar for the future?
doctrine.publication: Syracuse Journal of International Law and Commerce
doctrine.relevance: Key doctrinal analysis of Art. 12 equal legal capacity and the incompatibility of substituted decision-making regimes; underpins NormTrace red flag analysis for guardianship provisions.

doctrine.title: The United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities and the right to be free from nonconsensual psychiatric interventions
doctrine.publication: Syracuse Journal of International Law and Commerce
doctrine.relevance: Analysis of CRPD Arts. 12, 14, 15, and 17 as prohibiting non-consensual psychiatric treatment and disability-based deprivation of liberty; directly supports NormTrace Art. 14 incompatibility analysis.

doctrine.title: The politics of disablement
doctrine.relevance: Foundational articulation of the social model of disability (barriers rather than impairments as source of disadvantage); underpins CRPD's conceptual framework and NormTrace's analytical premises.

doctrine.title: A new human rights model of disability
doctrine.publication: In V. Della Fina, R. Cera and G. Palmisano (eds.), The United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities: A Commentary, Springer
doctrine.relevance: Articulates the human rights model of disability as extending and integrating the social model; key theoretical reference for understanding the CRPD's normative architecture.

doctrine.title: Los fundamentos de los derechos fundamentales
doctrine.relevance: Structural theory of fundamental rights as unconditional norms — relevant to NormTrace's analysis of declaratory vs. justiciable rights and the distinction between norms and metanorms.

doctrine.title: Los derechos sociales como derechos exigibles
doctrine.relevance: Framework for analysing the justiciability and enforceability of social rights; directly applicable to NormTrace's analysis of implementation gaps and declaratory provisions in Latin American legal systems.

doctrine.title: El control de convencionalidad en la jurisprudencia de la Corte Interamericana de Derechos Humanos
doctrine.relevance: Systematic analysis of the conventionality control doctrine in the Inter-American system; essential for understanding Mexico-specific application in NormTrace Stage 3.

doctrine.title: El modelo social de discapacidad: orígenes, caracterización y plasmación en la Convención Internacional sobre los Derechos de las Personas con Discapacidad
doctrine.relevance: Comprehensive Spanish-language analysis of the social model and its embodiment in the CRPD; primary reference for Spanish-language NormTrace outputs on the conceptual framework.

doctrine.title: The UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities: European and Scandinavian perspectives
doctrine.relevance: European perspectives on CRPD implementation; relevant to NormTrace Swiss analysis given Scandinavian and continental European legal traditions.

doctrine.title: The development of disability rights under international law: from charity to human rights
doctrine.relevance: Comprehensive analysis of the trajectory from charitable to rights-based approaches in international disability law; supports NormTrace's language model classification (rights-based / assistentialist).
